## [Canon’s New Sweet Spot: How Much Resolution Do Hybrid Shooters Really Need?](https://camerajabber.com/photography-news/canons-new-sweet-spot-how-much-resolution-do-hybrid-shooters-really-need/)
*Camera Jabber* | 22/01/2026 | Score: 0.70
Tags: model_release, evaluation, api_update, photography, camera

> We have all heard it at some time or another – the belief that that more megapixels translate to higher image quality is one virtually every photographer or smartphone shooter will be familiar with. It was this assumption that paved the way for the colloquial ‘megapixel race’ back in the early 2000s, where camera companies like Nikon and Canon often touted the number of pixels as a marketing tool to sell new models. Today, it is widely accepted that the megapixel race concluded sometime around 2010. From then on, camera companies vowed to shift their focal points to improving other aspects of image quality, low-light performance, and new features. Around this time, camera companies had begun to accept that cramming as many pixels onto a sensor as possible would often create more problems than it would solve, giving rise to issues like noise and poor low-light performance.&#160; Effectively, they realised...

> Glyph Production Technologies has unveiled the Atom EVX, a new line of ultra-fast portable SSDs engineered for demanding creative professionals working with high-resolution video, RAW photography, and field production. With speeds reaching up to 2,800MB/s, the Atom EVX series combines cutting-edge NVMe performance with rugged reliability in a palm-sized aluminium enclosure. Designed to meet the data-intensive needs of content creators shooting in 8K, ProRes, RAW, or multi-cam formats, the Atom EVX uses USB-C (USB 3.2 Gen 2&#215;2) to maximise bandwidth. Whether offloading massive video files or editing directly from the drive, it promises smooth, stutter-free performance—especially valuable for workflows on set or on location. The drive is built around an NVMe solid-state platform housed in a thermally efficient, MIL-SPEC-rated aluminium shell, offering robust protection against heat, drops, vibration, and the rigours of travel. *Camera Jabber* | 24/01/2026 | Score: 0.70
Tags: model_release, hardware, creative_tool, creative_workflow, announcement, photography, camera, lens

> Viltrox has announced a groundbreaking new product for filmmakers and vintage lens enthusiasts: the NexusFocus F1, a high-precision autofocus adapter that transforms manual PL-mount cinema lenses into intelligent, autofocus-capable tools on Sony E-mount cameras. Launched via Kickstarter on January 15th, the NexusFocus F1 is the first adapter of its kind to offer full FIZ (focus, iris, zoom) motor control powered by AI subject recognition and Sony’s phase-detection autofocus (PDAF) system. This compact yet robust adapter allows creators to integrate legendary cine optics into modern autofocus workflows, opening new creative possibilities for solo shooters, run-and-gun filmmakers, and small crews. By pairing classic manual lenses with Sony’s advanced subject-tracking features, the NexusFocus F1 eliminates the need for external LiDAR systems, making AI autofocus more accessible and efficient in dynamic shooting environments. Constructed from aluminium alloy and stainless steel, the unit weighs just 262g and features a dust-resistant mount, USB-C power port, and...

> Fujifilm has announced the release of the instax mini Link+, a portable smartphone printer designed to give users more control, creativity, and convenience when bringing their digital photos to life. As the latest addition to the successful instax mini Link series, the mini Link+ introduces a host of upgraded features through the instax mini Link™ app, combining tactile instant prints with bold, modern design. Whether pulling images from a phone’s camera roll or favourite Pinterest board, the Simple Print mode allows users to print photos on demand. Those looking to fine-tune every shot can explore the new Simulation Mode, which previews how prints will look in various real-world settings—ideal for home décor planning or curated displays. The enhanced Design Print Mode ensures that photos, text, illustrations, and graphics are rendered with sharp detail, while the Frame Print feature allows for the addition of fun overlays, captions, or stickers. Designed for solo videographers and mirrorless creators, the RS 5 introduces a significant upgrade in tracking, stability, and operating efficiency, while also improving runtime and compatibility within DJI’s broader production ecosystem. Check out the full DJI Ronin RS 5 review here at Camera Jabber The star feature of the RS 5 is the RS Enhanced Intelligent Tracking Module, an evolution of DJI’s earlier tracking system that now allows videographers to frame and follow subjects directly via the gimbal’s touchscreen. The system can track not just people but also vehicles, pets, or inanimate objects, locking onto subjects up to 10 metres away. If a subject leaves the frame, reacquisition is automatic. *Camera Jabber* | 05/02/2026 | Score: 0.70
Tags: product_launch, announcement, photography, camera, lens, photography_technique

> Intent on expanding its professional RF lens range, Canon has announced the launch of two new L-series products: the RF 14mm F1.4L VCM and the RF 7-14mm F2.8-3.5L FISHEYE STM, the latter being billed as the world’s first 190° fisheye zoom lens. Capable of delivering two distinctive looks when paired with a full-frame EOS R System, the RF 7-14mm F2.8-3.5L FISHEYE STM offers an ultra-wide 190°circular fisheye image at 7mm, as well as a frame-filling 180° diagonal fisheye image at 14mm. While over on APS-C cameras, the maximum wide angle can be set to approximately 8.7mm for 180° diagonal fisheye images. What’s more, circular fisheye videos captured on a compatible camera can also be converted to 2D 180° VR files via EOS VR Utility, allowing for immersive viewing via a VR headset.
